title: Garbage collection of data blocks in a storage system with direct-mapped storage devices
abstract: An indication to perform a garbage collection process for multiple erase blocks at a storage array that includes multiple storage devices may be received. Characteristics for each of the erase blocks may further be received. Valid data at each of the erase blocks may be identified. Furthermore, valid data from different erase blocks of the multiple erase blocks may be grouped based on a similarity of the plurality of characteristics between the valid data from the different erase blocks. The valid data from the different erase blocks may subsequently be stored at another erase block at the storage array.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A system comprising: a solid-state storage array comprising a plurality of direct-mapped solid-state storage devices; and a storage controller coupled to the solid-state storage array and external to the plurality of direct-mapped solid-state storage devices, the storage controller comprising a processing device, the processing device to: receive an indication to perform a garbage collection process for the solid-state storage array that includes the plurality of direct-mapped solid-state storage devices; in response to the indication to perform the garbage collection process, receive a plurality of characteristics associated with a plurality of erase blocks of the plurality of direct-mapped solid-state storage devices that are included in the solid-state storage array identify valid data from the plurality of erase blocks of the plurality of direct- mapped solid-state storage devices that are included in the solid-state storage array; and perform the garbage collection process for the solid-state storage array by grouping valid data from the plurality of erase blocks based on a similarity of the plurality of characteristics between the valid data and storing the grouped valid data at another erase block at the plurality of direct-mapped solid-state storage devices of the solid-state storage array. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein each of the plurality of direct-mapped solid-state storage devices does not include an intemal storage controller that performs another garbage collection process for a respective direct-mapped solid-state storage device. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the indication to perform the garbage collection process for the solid-state storage array is based on a capacity of the direct-mapped solid-state storage array being used by the solid-state storage array. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the plurality of characteristics specifies an age associated with each of the valid data. 5. The system of claim 4, wherein the age associated with a particular valid data corresponds to when the particular valid data was received in a write transaction by the solid- state storage array. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein the plurality of characteristics specifies a deduplication importance associated with each valid data, the deduplication importance corresponding to a number of times that a respective valid data was associated with a deduplication operation. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein the plurality of characteristics specifies a type of data for each of the valid data, the type of data corresponding to an identification of a respective valid data as user data or a particular type of metadata. 8. A method comprising: receiving an indication to perform a garbage collection process for a plurality of erase blocks at a storage array comprising a plurality of storage devices; receiving a plurality of characteristics associated with each of the erase blocks; identifying valid data at each of the erase blocks; grouping, by a storage controller comprising a processing device and that is external from the plurality of storage devices, valid data from different erase blocks of the plurality of erase blocks based on a similarity of the plurality of characteristics between the valid data from the different erase blocks; and storing the valid data from the different erase blocks at another erase block at the storage array. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the indication to perform the garbage collection process is received when the storage array has exceeded a threshold amount of usage of the plurality of erase blocks, and wherein the garbage collection process corresponds to a removal of invalid data at the plurality of erase blocks. 10. The method of claim 8, wherein the processing device is further to: receive a priority value for each of the plurality of characteristics, and wherein the grouping of the valid data is based on the priority value for each of the plurality of characteristics. 11. The method of claim 8, wherein the plurality of storage devices correspond to direct- mapped storage devices that do not include an intemal storage controller that performs another garbage collection process for a respective direct-mapped storage device. 12. The method of claim 8, wherein the plurality of characteristics comprises a characteristic that specifies an age associated with each of the valid data, the age corresponding to when a respective valid data has been received in a write transaction by the storage array. 13. The method of claim 8, wherein the plurality of characteristics comprises a characteristic that specifies a deduplication importance associated with each of the valid data, the deduplication importance corresponding to a number of times that a respective valid data has been associated with a deduplication operation. 14. The method of claim 8, wherein the plurality of characteristics comprises a characteristic that specifies a type of data for each of the valid data, the type of data corresponding to an identification of a respective valid data as user data or a particular type of metadata. 15. A non-transitory computer readable storage medium storing instructions, which when executed, cause a processing device to: receive an indication to perform a garbage collection process for a plurality of erase blocks at a storage array comprising a plurality of storage devices; receive a plurality of characteristics associated with each of the erase blocks; identify valid data at each of the erase blocks; group, by the processing device of a storage controller that is external from the plurality of storage devices, valid data from different erase blocks of the plurality of erase blocks based on a similarity of the plurality of characteristics between the valid data from the different erase blocks; and store the valid data from the different erase blocks at another erase block at the storage array. 16. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the indication to perform the garbage collection process is received when the storage array has exceeded a threshold amount of usage of the plurality of erase blocks, and wherein the garbage collection process corresponds to a removal of invalid data at the plurality of erase blocks. 17. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the processing device is further to: receive a priority value for each of the plurality of characteristics, and wherein the grouping of the valid data is based on the priority value for each of the plurality of characteristics. 18. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the plurality of storage devices correspond to direct-mapped storage devices that do not include an intemal storage controller that performs another garbage collection process for a respective direct- mapped storage device. 19. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the plurality of characteristics comprises a characteristic that specifies an age associated with each of the valid data, the age corresponding to when a respective valid data has been received in a write transaction by the storage array. 20. The non-transitory computer readable storage medium of claim 15, wherein the plurality of characteristics comprises a characteristic that specifies a deduplication importance associated with each of the valid data, the deduplication importance corresponding to a number of times that a respective valid data has been associated with a deduplication operation.
